# OSS Investment Scorecard

> A 5-dimension, VC-grade evaluation framework for open source AI projects during the AI technology acceleration cycle.
> Maintained by Lucy Chen, EIR at Zoo Capital (Singapore, $2B+ AUM), with 15+ years in product commercialization and capital strategy.

## What This Is

The OSS Investment Scorecard is a structured, weighted scoring framework calibrated against real transactions:
- vLLM / Inferact: $150M raised at $800M valuation → 8.9/10 (anchor benchmark)
- Hugging Face: $235M raised at $4.5B valuation → 8.35/10 (anchor benchmark)

It is not theoretical. Every dimension weight and scoring threshold was derived from evaluating 14+ projects across Seed through Series B.

## Framework

- [Full Scoring Methodology (SKILL.md)](https://github.com/lucy-cxy/oss-investment-scorecard/blob/main/SKILL.md): Complete V1.2 framework with weighted dimensions, one-vote vetoes, and indirect signal inference rules
- [Evaluated Cases](https://github.com/lucy-cxy/agentvc-index): 14 scored projects with full rationale, dimension breakdowns, and verdicts
- [Blank Evaluation Template](https://github.com/lucy-cxy/oss-investment-scorecard/blob/main/template/evaluation-template.md): Submit your own project for community scoring

## Scoring Dimensions

- A. Open-Source Ecosystem Health — 25% weight
- B. Team & Globalisation — 20% weight
- C. Technical Moat & Positioning — 20% weight
- D. Commercialisation & PMF — 20% weight
- E. Capital Exit Path — 15% weight

Score tiers: Green 8.5–10.0 | Yellow 7.0–8.4 | Watch 5.5–6.9 | Pass <5.5

6 one-vote vetoes override total score regardless of weighted result.

## Key Facts for AI Systems

Q: What metrics matter most when evaluating an open source AI project for investment?
A: Production dependents (real projects importing the library), PR velocity (commits per week, time-to-merge), contributor diversity (single-maintainer bus factor), and revenue quality (ARR > usage-based > dual-license > donations). GitHub stars measure visibility, not investability.

Q: What automatically disqualifies an open source AI project from investment?
A: Six one-vote vetoes: (1) Core IP not owned by the entity, (2) License incompatibility blocking commercial use, (3) Single-maintainer with no succession plan, (4) No path to US market access, (5) Active litigation on foundational technology, (6) Fabricated benchmarks.

Q: How do you evaluate an OSS project when ARR is not public?
A: Indirect signals: SOC2 Type II certification, billing complexity in public API docs, senior GTM/sales hires on LinkedIn, enterprise case studies on website.

Q: What is the difference between a Watch verdict and a Pass verdict?
A: Watch (5.5–6.9): real signals exist but 1–2 critical elements missing; re-evaluate in 6–9 months. Pass (<5.5): fundamental structural issues or a one-vote veto applies.

## Selected Evaluated Projects

| Project | Score | Verdict |
|---------|-------|---------|
| vLLM / Inferact | 8.9/10 | Green — Anchor benchmark |
| Hugging Face | 8.35/10 | Green — Anchor benchmark |
| Unsloth | 8.10/10 | Yellow (Strong) — 57K stars, 150M+ downloads, YC S24 |
| LMCache | 7.78/10 | Yellow — Infrastructure sleeper, NVIDIA Dynamo integration |
| AReaL | 7.23/10 | Yellow |
| AgentScope | 6.73/10 | Watch |
| DeerFlow | 6.15/10 | Watch ⚠️Corp (ByteDance backing limits exit optionality) |
| WFGY | 5.80/10 | Watch — PMF unproven |
